reasoning: Brazil's extreme poverty fell dramatically from 2022 to 2024 due to expanded social programs (Bolsa Família) and an improved labor market. In the 2024 G20 publication, Brazil was 19th (behind Indonesia at 18th). Given the sharp drop in Brazil's poverty rate (the national series saw extreme poverty drop by over 40% from 2022 to 2024), Brazil's international metric is highly likely to have fallen below Indonesia's 2.5%, moving Brazil to 18th place or better, assuming Argentina's poverty crisis doesn't also push Argentina below Brazil. The main uncertainty pushing the probability down towards 50% is whether the IBGE will officially publish an updated G20 ranking "Caderno" by September 2026, as the 2024 publication was closely tied to Brazil's G20 presidency. If no comparable ranking is updated, the question may resolve ambiguous or negative. I account for the strong fundamentals favoring Brazil by assigning a slightly positive lean.
